eval-1-finance-dashboard: WITH: Excellent — bottom nav, safe areas, dvh, chart summary, 44px targets all present. WITHOUT: Used hamburger instead of bottom nav, no safe-area handling, no chart summary, tap targets below 44px. Strong differentiation.
eval-2-checkout-form: WITH: All 6 assertions pass including enterKeyHint chaining, WebKit bug references, and safe-area-aware submit. WITHOUT: Got the basics right (single-column, inputMode, inline submit) but missed enterKeyHint and dvh. Moderate differentiation — baseline Sonnet already knows inputMode patterns.
eval-3-analytics-table: WITH: One fail — action buttons at h-9 (36px) miss the 44px assertion even with skill guidance. Toolbar buttons correctly hit 44px. Consider strengthening the skill's emphasis on ALL interactive elements meeting 44px, not just toolbar-level items. WITHOUT: Actions hidden behind expand toggle, all tap targets undersized, toolbar icons too small.
eval-4-product-listing: WITH: Perfect score. Every skill-specific pattern present: always-visible CTA, 44px wishlist button, filter sheet, enlarged checkboxes, dvh, iOS auto-zoom prevention. WITHOUT: Got the big patterns right (CTA visible, drawer filters, 2-col grid) but missed 44px on wishlist and checkbox targets.
eval-5-app-layout: WITH: Clean conversion — hamburger removed, bottom tab bar added, safe areas, dvh, risk-prioritized audit. WITHOUT: Added bottom nav BUT kept the hamburger + drawer alongside it — duplicate navigation systems for the same 5 items. This is a UX anti-pattern the skill explicitly prevents. Weakest differentiation otherwise since baseline caught viewport-fit and dvh.
